Published脗Jul 27, 2024, 02:38 AM

Updated脗Jul 27, 2024, 06:24 AM

PARIS - French President Emmanuel Macron declared the Olympic Games open on July 26 after a soaking wet ceremony in which athletes were cheered by the crowd along the Seine, dancers took to the roofs of Paris and Lady Gaga sang a French cabaret song.

France芒聙聶s three-time Olympic gold medallists Marie-Jose Perec and Teddy Riner then lit the Olympic cauldron, suspended on a hot-air balloon, before Canada芒聙聶s Celine Dion sang Edith Piaf芒聙聶s Hymn To Love, in her first public performance in years, drawing huge cheers from the crowd.

A fleet of barges took the competitors on a 6km stretch of the river alongside some of the French capital芒聙聶s most famous landmarks, as performers recreated some of the sports to be showcased in the Games on floating platforms.

It was the first time that an opening ceremony has taken place outside a stadium, adding to the headaches for a vast security operation, just hours aftera sabotage attack on the high-speed TGV rail networkcaused travel chaos across France.

芒聙聹I invite everybody: dream with us. Like the Olympic athletes, be inspired with the joy that only sport can give us. Let us celebrate this Olympic spirit of living in peace,芒聙聺 International Olympics Committee president Thomas Bach said, as the ceremony came to an end at the foot of the Eiffel Tower.

More than 10,500 athletes will compete at the Olympics, 100 years since Paris last staged the Games.

Competition started on July 24 and the first of the 329 gold medals will be awarded on July 27.

As the show started four hours earlier, a giant plume of blue, white and red smoke, resembling the French flag, was sent high above a bridge over the Seine as part of a show that included many postcard-like depictions of France, including a huge cancan line performed by Moulin Rouge dancers on the banks.

A more modern image of the country was on display when French-Malian pop star Aya Nakamura, the most-listened to French female singer in the world, sang some of her biggest hits, accompanied by the French Republican Guard芒聙聶s army choir.

Nakamura芒聙聶s performance drew some of the ceremony芒聙聶s biggest cheers.

Rumours of her inclusion had sparked a row over French identity, with supporters saying said she represented the vibrancy of modern-day France while her detractors saying her music owes more to foreign influences than French.

While the celebration of French culture, fashion and history was warmly cheered by many of the 300,000 spectators lining the river, hundreds were seen leaving early as the rain fell.

Singapore芒聙聶s athletes aboard a boat in the floating parade on the River Seine.

PHOTO: REUTERS

芒聙聹It was good other than the rain, it was nice, it was different, instead of being in a stadium being on the river, so that芒聙聶s always a good thing - interesting, unique,芒聙聺 said Avid Pureval, 34, who came to the Games from Ohio.

芒聙聹Once you芒聙聶re wet, it芒聙聶s fine,芒聙聺 he said. Still, he was heading back to his hotel after the French boat passed, long before the ceremony ended.

芒聙聹It would have been better with sun,芒聙聺 said Josephine, from Paris, sitting beside her nine-year-old daughter and who paid 芒聜卢1,600 (S$2,300) for her seat.

Athletes of Germany, Algeria, Albania and Afghanistan pass Paris芒聙聶  Eiffel Tower aboard a boat, in the Olympics opening ceremony.

PHOTO: REUTERS

With many world leaders and VIPs present, the ceremony was protected by snipers on rooftops.

The Seine芒聙聶s riverbed was swept for bombs, and Paris芒聙聶 airspace was closed.

Some 45,000 police and thousands of soldiers were deployed in a huge security operation in Paris for the ceremony.

Armed police patrolled along the river in inflatable boats as the armada made its passage along the Seine.

A mix of French and international stars, including soccer great Zinedine Zidane, 14-times French Open champion Rafa Nadal, 23-times Grand Slam champion Serena Williams and three paralympic athletes were among the last torchbearers before the cauldron was lit.

It will blaze until the closing ceremony on Aug 11.

At the start of the parade, applause erupted for the Greek boat - the first delegation, by tradition - and there were even bigger cheers for the boat that followed, carrying the refugees芒聙聶 team. The French, US and Ukrainian delegations also got loud cheers.

The two most decorated athletes in the Games芒聙聶 history, Michael Phelps and Martin Fourcade, unveiled the gold, silver and bronze medals.

Boats moving along the River Seine in Paris, during the opening ceremony, watched by spectators.

PHOTO: REUTERS

At one point, there was a live crossover to the early morning welcome ceremony at the surfing venue, 16,000km away in the Pacific island of Tahiti.

France is at its highest level of security, though officials have repeatedly said there was no specific threat to the opening ceremony or the Games.

But since the last Games - the Winter Olympics held in Beijing in 2022 - wars have erupted in Ukraine and Gaza, providing a tense international backdrop.

Spectators watching the July 26 opening ceremony.

PHOTO: REUTERS

Israeli competitors are being escorted by elite tactical units to and from events and given 24-hour protection throughout the Olympics due to the war in Gaza, officials say.

The Israel delegation got some boos, but also a lot of cheers, as it sailed by spectators, Reuters reporters saw. Chants of 芒聙聹Palestine! Palestine! Palestine!芒聙聺 rose from the crowd as the boat passed.

Macron, who won a second mandate two years ago, had hoped the Olympics would cement his legacy. But his failed bet on a snap legislative election has weakened him and cast a shadow over his moment on the international stage. REUTERS

Ukrainian flagbearers Mykhailo Romanchuk and Elina Svitolina (right) aboard a boat in the floating parade.

PHOTO: REUTERS
